seoTitle: How to Get Your Webhook Signing Secret in Stripe Safely
metaDescription: Find and copy your Stripe Webhook Signing Secret to verify payment events securely. This guide covers creating an endpoint and protecting the secret.
intro: Stripe webhooks let your app receive real-time updates on payments, subscriptions, and refunds, but they only work correctly once you've set up a webhook endpoint and grabbed the Signing Secret. This guide covers creating that endpoint, locating the secret in your dashboard, and copying it safely into your code. It also covers a few best practices so the secret doesn't end up exposed somewhere it shouldn't be.

steps.heading: Understand why the Webhook Secret matters
steps.body: Stripe uses the Webhook Signing Secret to verify that incoming events genuinely came from Stripe, which protects your app from spoofed requests.

steps.heading: Create a new webhook endpoint
steps.body: In the Developers, Webhooks section of your Stripe dashboard, set up a new endpoint pointing to the URL where your app will receive events.

steps.heading: Copy the Webhook Signing Secret
steps.body: Once the endpoint is created, copy the Signing Secret shown for that endpoint so you can use it to verify incoming webhook payloads.

steps.heading: Add the secret to your app or code
steps.body: Store the Webhook Secret securely in your app's environment configuration rather than hardcoding it directly into your source files.

steps.heading: Follow security best practices
steps.body: Keep the secret out of version control and client-side code, and test your webhook to confirm events are being verified correctly.

faq.question: Where do I find my Webhook Signing Secret in Stripe?
faq.answer: It's shown on the details page of the specific webhook endpoint you created, under the Developers, Webhooks section of your Stripe dashboard.

faq.question: Is the Webhook Secret the same as my Stripe API key?
faq.answer: No, the Webhook Signing Secret is specific to verifying webhook events and is separate from your Publishable or Secret API keys.

faq.question: What happens if I don't use the Webhook Secret to verify events?
faq.answer: Without verification, your endpoint can't confirm that incoming requests are genuinely from Stripe, which leaves it open to spoofed payloads.

faq.question: Can I have a different Webhook Secret for each endpoint?
faq.answer: Yes, each webhook endpoint you create in Stripe gets its own unique Signing Secret.
